items.headline: Joel Eriksson Ek quietly making impact for Minnesota in 2026 playoffs
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Inside the Minnesota Wild dressing room, the players know about Joel Eriksson Ek and his impact on the team. The center's current and previous coaches know, too. So do the media covering him every day.
"He's just special," Wild forward Matt Boldy said. "You don't find

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres Tampa Bay Lightning Montreal Canadiens 2026 playoff lookahead
items.description:
The Buffalo Sabres will play the Montreal Canadiens or Tampa Bay Lightning in the Eastern Conference Second Round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
The Sabres won their first playoff series since 2006-07 with a 4-1 victory against the Boston Bruins in Game 6 of the first round at TD Garden

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres Boston Bruins game 6 recap May 1 2026
items.description: BOSTON -- The Buffalo Sabres won a Stanley Cup Playoff series for the first time since 2007, defeating the Boston Bruins 4-1 in Game 6 of the Eastern Conference First Round at TD Garden on Friday.

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning Montreal Canadiens game 6 recap May 1 2026
items.description: MONTREAL -- Gage Goncalves scored at 9:03 of overtime for the Tampa Bay Lightning, who avoided elimination with a 1-0 win against the Montreal Canadiens in Game 6 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Bell Centre on Friday.

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning, Montreal Canadiens Game 7 lookahead
items.description: The Tampa Bay Lightning and Montreal Canadiens will play Game 7 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Benchmark International Arena on Sunday (TBD).

items.headline: Logan Stankoven flourishing in playoffs again for Hurricanes
items.description:
Logan Stankoven wore out a path to the podium in the first round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
Now, the Carolina Hurricanes forward is looking for more of the same in the second round when the Hurricanes open a best-of-7 series against the Philadelphia Flyers with Game 1 of the

items.headline: Interim coach DJ Smith to be candidate for Los Angeles Kings job
items.description:
EL SEGUNDO, Calif. -- D.J. Smith will be a candidate for the Los Angeles Kings coaching position next season, general manager Ken Holland said Friday.
Smith finished the season as interim coach after replacing Jim Hiller, who was fired on March 1.
“Certainly going to talk to a number of

items.headline: Short Shifts Power Rankings May 1 2026
items.description: Throw out the Xs and Os and join Short Shifts for our own weekly power rankings. What is our criteria? Whatever makes us smile, laugh, cry or any other wholesome feeling. This list is updated every Friday with our top moments of the week.

items.headline: St. Louis Blues sign Dylan Holloway to five year contract
items.description:
Dylan Holloway signed a five-year, $38.75 million contract with the St. Louis Blues on Friday. It has an average annual value of $7.75 million.
The 24-year-old forward missed time with injury this season but was still second on the Blues with 22 goals and tied for second on the team

items.description:

items.headline: Evgeni Malkin wants to stay in NHL, even if he leaves Pittsburgh
items.description:
CRANBERRY, Pa. -- Evgeni Malkin does not plan to retire from the NHL, even if it means parting with the Pittsburgh Penguins.
The three-time Stanley Cup-winning forward could become an unrestricted free agent on July 1 after completing the final season of a four-year, $24.4 million contract signed on July

items.headline: Cam York gets OT goal stick back from Philadelphia Flyers fan
items.description:
VOORHEES, N.J. -- Cam York used a golf analogy to explain how the stick he used to score the series-clinching overtime goal for the Philadelphia Flyers against the Pittsburgh Penguins in Game 6 of the Eastern Conference First Round on Wednesday ended up in the Xfinity Mobile Arena stands.
